questions.answer: A matrix is a rectangular arrangement of numbers (real or complex) in the form of rows and columns.
key_points: Matrix elements are enclosed in square brackets

questions.q: What is the order of a matrix?
questions.answer: The order of a matrix is defined as the number of rows and columns in the matrix.
key_points: Order = number of rows × number of columns

questions.answer: A square matrix is a matrix in which the number of rows is equal to the number of columns.
key_points: Diagonal elements are equal in a square matrix

questions.q: What is the transpose of a matrix?
questions.answer: The transpose of a matrix is obtained by interchanging its rows into columns or columns into rows.

questions.q: What is the adjoint of a matrix?
questions.answer: The adjoint of a matrix is the matrix obtained by taking the transpose of the cofactor matrix.
key_points: adj(A) = transpose of cofactor matrix of A

questions.q: State the property of the adjoint of a matrix.
questions.answer: The product of a matrix and its adjoint is equal to the determinant of the matrix multiplied by the identity matrix.
key_points: where I is the identity matrix

questions.q: State the property of the inverse of a matrix.
questions.answer: The inverse of a matrix is obtained by dividing the adjoint of the matrix by the determinant of the matrix.
key_points: where |A| is the determinant of A

questions.q: Find the transpose of the matrix A = [[1, 2], [3, 4]].
Find the adjoint of the matrix A = [[1, 2], [3, 4]].
key_points: adj(A) = transpose of cofactor matrix of A

questions.q: Find the inverse of the matrix A = [[1, 2], [3, 4]].
key_points: A is a non-square matrix, so inverse is not defined

questions.q: State the property of the determinant of a matrix.
questions.answer: The determinant of a matrix is a scalar value that can be used to determine the solvability of a system of linear equations.

questions.q: Find the determinant of the matrix A = [[1, 2], [3, 4]].
Find the solution of the system of linear equations 2x + 3y = 7 and x - 2y = -3 using matrices.
key_points: Use matrix inversion method to solve the system of linear equations
questions.topic: Solution of a System of Linear Equations

questions.q: State the property of the rank of a matrix.
questions.answer: The rank of a matrix is the maximum number of linearly independent rows or columns in the matrix.
key_points: where r is the maximum number of linearly independent rows or columns

questions.q: Find the rank of the matrix A = [[1, 2], [3, 4]].
